gloss.en: in a way showing little hope and great need

words.example: She looked desperately around for a weapon.

gloss.en: a feeling of having no hope, leading to risky action

words.example: In desperation, she called for help.

gloss.en: to dislike and have no respect for someone

words.example: I despise people who tell lies.

gloss.en: what will happen to you in the future; fate

words.example: He believed it was his destiny to lead.

gloss.en: the act of destroying something

words.example: We must stop the destruction of the rainforests.

gloss.en: to separate one thing from another

words.example: Please detach the form and send it back.

gloss.en: the state of not being involved or emotionally affected

words.example: She watched the scene with cool detachment.

gloss.en: a small individual fact or item

words.example: The trip was planned down to the last detail.

gloss.en: giving many details and lots of information

words.example: She gave a detailed description of the events.

gloss.en: to discover or notice something hard to see

words.example: The tests are designed to detect the disease early.

gloss.en: the quality of continuing to try despite difficulty

words.example: She showed great determination to finish the race.

gloss.en: having firmly decided to do something

words.example: She was determined to win the prize.

gloss.en: a word like 'the' or 'this' used before a noun

words.example: The word 'some' is a determiner.

gloss.en: poor and trying to grow its economy

words.example: Many developing countries need more clean water.

gloss.en: to give time or effort fully to something

words.example: She decided to devote her life to helping others.

gloss.en: loving someone or something very much

words.example: He is a devoted father to his children.

gloss.en: an illness with frequent, watery bowel movements

words.example: He got diarrhea after eating bad food.

gloss.en: an illness with frequent, watery bowel movements

words.example: The child had diarrhoea for two days.

gloss.en: to be different from something else

words.example: My views differ from yours on this point.

gloss.en: to make a hole in the ground

words.example: They had to dig a hole for the tree.

gloss.en: to break down food in your body

words.example: It takes time to digest a heavy meal.

gloss.en: to make something seem more worthy or serious

words.example: I won't dignify that question with an answer.

gloss.en: not bright; hard to see clearly

words.example: The room was lit by a dim lamp.

gloss.en: a US coin worth ten cents

words.example: He gave the boy a dime for the candy.

gloss.en: a serious infectious throat disease

words.example: A vaccine protects children from diphtheria.

gloss.en: a certificate showing you completed a course

words.example: She earned a diploma in nursing.

gloss.en: a condition that limits what someone can do

words.example: The building has ramps for people with a disability.

gloss.en: having a condition that limits some activities

words.example: The car park has spaces for disabled drivers.

gloss.en: a situation where people have different opinions

words.example: There was a disagreement about how to plan the show.

gloss.en: to make someone sad by not meeting their hopes

words.example: I don't want to disappoint my parents.

gloss.en: a feeling of sadness when hopes are not met

words.example: Book early for the show to avoid disappointment.

gloss.en: a feeling that something is bad or wrong

words.example: She frowned in disapproval at his rude words.

gloss.en: to think something is bad or wrong

words.example: My parents disapprove of my new friends.

gloss.en: a thin flat round object

words.example: He wears an identity disc around his neck.

gloss.en: a follower of a teacher or set of beliefs

words.example: The teacher had many loyal disciples.

gloss.en: training people to obey rules and behave well

words.example: The school has a reputation for strict discipline.

gloss.en: to break the link between two things

words.example: Please disconnect the printer before cleaning it.

gloss.en: an amount taken off the usual price

words.example: Students can get a discount on bus tickets.

gloss.en: to try to stop someone from doing something

words.example: They run a campaign to discourage smoking among teenagers.

gloss.en: to make someone or something seem less believable

words.example: His rivals tried to discredit him with lies.

gloss.en: to treat some people unfairly compared to others

words.example: It is wrong to discriminate against older workers.

gloss.en: the loss of respect because of bad behaviour

words.example: He was sent home in disgrace.

gloss.en: clothes or a look that hide who you are

words.example: The spy wore a clever disguise.

gloss.en: not honest; trying to trick people

words.example: Beware of dishonest traders in tourist areas.

gloss.en: the act of lying or trying to trick people

words.example: He lost his job because of his dishonesty.

gloss.en: to show someone that a belief is false

words.example: I hate to disillusion you, but it won't work.

gloss.en: a thin flat round object

words.example: Red blood cells are roughly the shape of a disk.

gloss.en: to decide something is not worth considering

words.example: He was quick to dismiss the idea as silly.

gloss.en: an illness affecting part of the body or mind

words.example: She suffers from a rare blood disorder.

gloss.en: messy or behaving in an uncontrolled way

words.example: He was arrested for disorderly behaviour.

gloss.en: badly planned or not arranged well

words.example: The meeting was completely disorganised.

gloss.en: badly planned or not arranged well

words.example: His desk was messy and disorganized.

gloss.en: not needed; able to be done without

words.example: In a crisis, comfort becomes dispensable.

gloss.en: an arrangement of things for people to see

words.example: The shop had a beautiful flower display.

gloss.en: a feeling of being annoyed or not pleased

words.example: She showed her displeasure with a cold look.

gloss.en: a person's natural character or mood

words.example: She has a cheerful disposition.

gloss.en: to stop someone taking part for breaking rules

words.example: The runner was disqualified for cheating.

gloss.en: a lack of care or attention to something

words.example: He showed a total disregard for the rules.

gloss.en: a feeling of not being happy with something

words.example: Workers expressed dissatisfaction with their pay.

gloss.en: not happy because something is not good enough

words.example: Many customers were dissatisfied with the service.

gloss.en: a person who openly opposes the government

words.example: The dissident was jailed for his views.

gloss.en: far away in space or time

words.example: We heard the distant sound of music.

gloss.en: clearly different or easily noticed

words.example: There was a distinct smell of gas.

gloss.en: to recognize the difference between things

words.example: Children must learn to distinguish right from wrong.

gloss.en: very successful and respected

words.example: She is a distinguished scientist.

gloss.en: a change that makes something untrue or unclear

words.example: The report was a distortion of the facts.

gloss.en: to take someone's attention away from something

words.example: Don't distract me while I'm driving.

gloss.en: something that takes your attention away

words.example: The noise was a constant distraction.

gloss.en: making you feel very upset or worried
gloss.zh: 令人痛苦的；令人忧虑的

words.example: The news was deeply distressing.

gloss.en: to give or share things among many people

words.example: The leaflets have been widely distributed.

gloss.en: the way something is spread over an area

words.example: They studied the distribution of the disease.

gloss.en: a person or company that supplies goods to shops

words.example: They are the main distributor of the product.

gloss.en: an area of a country or town

words.example: She works in the city's financial district.

gloss.en: to interrupt someone working, sleeping, etc.

words.example: I'm sorry to disturb you, but can I ask something?

gloss.en: a head-first jump into deep water

words.example: She made a perfect dive into the pool.

gloss.en: very different from each other; of many kinds

words.example: People from diverse cultures came to the event.

gloss.en: a change to a different route or focus

words.example: A diversion sent traffic down a side road.

gloss.en: a range of many different people or things

words.example: We value the diversity of the rainforest.

gloss.en: a difference that separates two groups

words.example: He worked to bridge the cultural divide.

gloss.en: split into separate parts or disagreeing groups

words.example: The country was divided over the issue.

gloss.en: the act of dividing something into parts

words.example: The cell grows by cell division.

gloss.en: the legal ending of a marriage

words.example: Their marriage ended in divorce.

gloss.en: the material in cells that carries genetic information

words.example: Police found his dna at the scene.

gloss.en: a place where ships are loaded or repaired

words.example: The ship arrived at the dock at noon.

gloss.en: an official paper or file with information

words.example: Please read and sign the attached document.

gloss.en: inside one country; not foreign

words.example: The airline runs many domestic flights.

gloss.en: more powerful or important than others

words.example: The firm has a dominant position in the market.

gloss.en: to control or have great influence over something

words.example: One team came to dominate the whole match.

gloss.en: to give money or goods, especially to charity

words.example: He donated thousands of pounds to charity.

gloss.en: something given to help a person or charity

words.example: She made a generous donation to the school.

gloss.en: an animal like a small horse with long ears

words.example: The farmer kept a donkey on his land.

gloss.en: death, ruin, or terrible events sure to come

words.example: A sense of doom hung over the city.

gloss.en: a person who guards the door of a building

words.example: The doorkeeper checked our tickets.

gloss.en: the opening where a door is

words.example: She stood in the doorway and waved.

gloss.en: a building where students sleep

words.example: He shares a dorm with three other students.

gloss.en: a building where students live and sleep

words.example: The new dormitory holds two hundred students.

gloss.en: a small round mark

words.example: There are dots above the letters i and j.

gloss.en: twice as much; in two ways

words.example: We must be doubly careful in the dark.

gloss.en: not certain; unlikely to happen

words.example: It is doubtful whether he will come.

gloss.en: a white bird often used as a sign of peace

words.example: A white dove is a symbol of peace.

gloss.en: to or at a lower place or position

words.example: She jumped down off the chair.

gloss.en: the loss of power, success, or wealth

words.example: His pride led to his downfall.

gloss.en: data copied from another computer system

words.example: The download took only a few seconds.

gloss.en: to make a company smaller by cutting jobs

words.example: The firm had to downsize to save money.

gloss.en: in or towards the centre of a city

words.example: We drove downtown to see a movie.

gloss.en: towards a lower place or position

words.example: She was lying face downwards on the grass.

gloss.en: money or goods a bride brings to a marriage

words.example: Her family gave a large dowry.

gloss.en: a group of twelve

words.example: Give me a dozen eggs, please.

gloss.en: a rough early version of a piece of writing

words.example: This is only the first draft of my speech.

gloss.en: to pull something along with effort

words.example: The sack is too heavy to lift, so drag it.

gloss.en: sudden, very great, and often surprising

words.example: There was a dramatic increase in prices.

gloss.en: suddenly and to a very great degree

words.example: Prices have fallen dramatically.

gloss.en: cloth hung in loose folds, as curtains

words.example: Heavy drapery covered the tall windows.

gloss.en: a flow of cold air in a room

words.example: I felt a cold draught from the open door.

gloss.en: to feel very afraid of something coming

words.example: I dread the thought of the exam.

gloss.en: very bad or unpleasant

words.example: The weather was dreadful all week.

gloss.en: thinking about pleasant things, not paying attention

words.example: She had a dreamy look in her eyes.

gloss.en: safe or pleasant to drink

words.example: The river water is not drinkable.

gloss.en: to bend or hang down weakly

words.example: The flowers began to droop in the heat.
